name: Alaska SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Alaska's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income individuals and families to purchase groceries. Alaska has higher income limits and benefit amounts due to high cost of living.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting Alaska SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Higher income limits than lower-48 states due to Alaska cost of living adjustments; generally 130% FPL with Alaska-specific thresholds

name: Alaska Medicaid (Denali KidCare)
description: Alaska's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital care, dental, vision, prescriptions, and behavioral health services. Denali KidCare covers children and pregnant women.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children (Denali KidCare): up to 208% FPL.

name: Alaska Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to Alaska workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Alaska Department of Labor and Workforce Development.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: Alaska Housing Finance Corporation (AHFC)
description: Alaska's housing finance agency providing rental assistance, public housing, homebuyer programs, weatherization, energy programs, and homelessness prevention across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income Alaska residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: Alaska Legal Services Corporation
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Alaskans including rural and remote communities, covering housing, family law, public benefits, tribal law, and domestic violence issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Alaska residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level (Alaska-adjusted)
languages: English, Yup'ik, and other Alaska Native languages via interpreters

name: Alaska Job Centers (AJC)
description: Provides workforce development services through local job center offices including job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, and connections to Alaska employers.
eligibilitySummary: All Alaska residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Alaska LIHEAP (Heating Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income Alaska households pay heating and energy costs. Especially critical in Alaska due to extreme winter temperatures and high energy costs in rural areas.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Alaska households with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 150% of federal poverty level (Alaska-adjusted)
hours: Seasonal program; applications typically open October

name: Alaska 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Alaskans to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, and crisis intervention resources.
eligibilitySummary: All Alaska residents seeking community resource information
languages: English, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Food Bank of Alaska
description: Alaska's Feeding America affiliate distributing food to over 200 partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and meal programs across the state. Operates mobile food pantries reaching remote communities.
eligibilitySummary: Any Alaska resident experiencing food insecurity

name: Alaska Office of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to Alaska veterans including benefits claims assistance, employment services, education benefits, and connections to VA healthcare and federal veterans programs.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska veterans and their eligible dependents

name: Alaska Careline (Crisis Intervention)
description: Alaska's statewide crisis hotline providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress. Also serves as the 988 Lifeline center for Alaska.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Alaska experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Alaska Child Care Assistance Program
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income Alaska families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children who meet income and employment requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 85% of state median income

name: Alaska Temporary Assistance Program (ATAP)
description: Alaska's TANF cash assistance program for low-income families with children, providing monthly benefits and employment services to help families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children meeting work participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; Alaska-specific poverty guidelines apply

name: Alaska WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level (Alaska-adjusted)

name: Alaska Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Alaska homes including insulation, air sealing, heating system repairs, and energy audits. Critical for Alaska's extreme climate.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level (Alaska-adjusted)

name: Alaska Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services to Alaskans with disabilities including career counseling, skills training, assistive technology, and job placement assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska residents with disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Alaska Permanent Fund Dividend (PFD)
description: Annual dividend paid to eligible Alaska residents from the state's Permanent Fund oil wealth savings account. Provides a significant annual payment to every qualifying resident.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska residents who have lived in the state for at least one full calendar year and intend to remain

name: Alaska Senior and Disabilities Services
description: Provides services for Alaska seniors and adults with disabilities including home and community-based waivers, personal care attendant services, adult day programs, and respite care.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska residents age 65+ and adults with physical or developmental disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; Medicaid eligibility may be required for some services

name: Alaska Domestic Violence Hotline (ANDVSA)
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault in Alaska.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence or sexual assault in Alaska
languages: English, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Anchorage People Mover
description: Public bus transit system serving the Anchorage metropolitan area with fixed-route and paratransit services. Reduced fares for seniors, youth, and persons with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: All Anchorage residents; reduced fares for seniors 60+, youth, and persons with disabilities

name: Municipality of Anchorage Community Development
description: Administers federal housing and community development programs in Anchorage including CDBG, HOME, and emergency housing programs for low-income residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income residents of Anchorage

name: Bean's Cafe (Anchorage)
description: Anchorage's largest free meal program serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner daily to anyone in need. Also provides overnight shelter services and resource referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Anchorage in need of a meal; no documentation required
hours: Breakfast, lunch, and dinner served daily

name: Fairbanks Community Food Bank
description: Provides emergency food distribution and community meals to individuals and families in the Fairbanks North Star Borough experiencing food insecurity.
eligibilitySummary: Fairbanks area residents experiencing food insecurity

name: Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium (ANTHC)
description: Provides comprehensive healthcare services to Alaska Native and American Indian people including primary care, dental, behavioral health, and traditional healing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska Native and American Indian beneficiaries

name: Disability Law Center of Alaska
description: Alaska's designated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services, abuse and neglect, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy

name: Alaska Head Start Association
description: Comprehensive early childhood education for low-income Alaska families providing education, health, nutrition, and family support for children birth to age 5, including programs in rural and tribal communities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5, with priority for Alaska Native families
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level (Alaska-adjusted)
languages: English, Yup'ik, and other Alaska Native languages

name: Alaska Adult Education
description: Provides free adult basic education, GED preparation, and ESL classes for Alaska adults seeking to improve their education and employment skills.
eligibilitySummary: Alaska adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: Alaska Power Cost Equalization Program
description: Unique Alaska program that subsidizes electricity costs in rural communities where power costs are significantly higher than the state average, helping reduce utility bills for rural residents.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of eligible rural Alaska communities with high electricity costs

name: Alaska Marine Highway System
description: State-operated ferry system connecting communities across coastal Alaska. Essential transportation for communities without road access. Reduced fares for Alaska residents.
eligibilitySummary: All travelers; Alaska residents receive reduced fares

name: AWARE (Aiding Women in Abuse and Rape Emergencies) - Juneau
description: Provides crisis intervention, emergency shelter, and supportive services for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault in the Juneau area, plus community outreach and prevention programs.
eligibilitySummary: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault in the Juneau area

name: Alaska General Relief Assistance
description: State-funded emergency cash assistance for very low-income Alaska adults without children who do not qualify for TANF, providing temporary aid for basic necessities.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income Alaska adults without dependent children who do not qualify for other programs
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by household size
